claim: The DPRK symbolic order constitutionally encodes frozen-pathology as a design principle: the state is defined as the application of the eternal leaders' ideas (passage 1), and the population is constitutionally bound to 'uphold the eternal leaders' and 'defend and carry forward their ideas' without revision (passage 2) — a constitutional prohibition on symbolic correction that maximizes symbolic-order strength while eliminating corrigibility by definition.

confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Both passages are from the same University of Hawaiʻi English translation of the 2019 DPRK Constitution. The constitutional encoding of the eternal-leaders principle is clearly present in the translation and consistent with all major scholarly accounts of North Korean ideology. Confidence capped at moderate: translation chain from Korean original is unverified; chain is limited to constitutional text without operational documentation of frozen-pathology in practice.
